title: nuphar lutea subsp. rubrodisca
intro: Nuphar lutea subsp. rubrodisca, also known as the yellow water lily or spatterdock, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Nymphaeaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its roots and rhizomes. The health benefits of Nuphar lutea subsp. rubrodisca include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even act as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its dried rhizomes can be used as a powder or added to other herbal remedies.
